summary: "greenwashing lawsuits, climate litigation, JBS, TotalEnergies, EnergyAustralia, Apple carbon neutral, Rosebank oilfield, Norwegian oilfields, Urgenda, ICJ advisory opinion, human right to healthy climate, net zero false claims, carbon offsets, meat industry, fossil fuel approval, scope 3 emissions, UK climate plan, coal"
---

#greenwashing #terratalk #meat 

Letitia James is perhaps best known as the New York Attorney General who recently won a US$450 million fraud case against former President Donald Trump and a landmark corruption case against the National Rifle Association.  

> Now James is taking on JBS , the world’s largest beef producer. The company's reputation precedes it: JBS has faced huge fines in the [United States](https://t.dripemail2.com/c/eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9._X19zPXo2ODJhNzg4czhnNjcyOHRmZnNxJnV0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZHJpcCZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj1OZXdzbGV0dGVyKy0rQXByaWwrMTUifQ.RdfOJxERGIUUIQWqp9B1S2_1Nk2AwOzrESpSimDivNQ) and [Brazil](https://t.dripemail2.com/c/eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..daI2koqijRNl2tJBN45ZvEE-9AFrid33B1YLWw1dJtU) for bribery and price-fixing schemes; more than a dozen U.S. senators have tried to block it from issuing stock on the New York Stock Exchange, [citing its](https://t.dripemail2.com/c/eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9._X19zPXo2ODJhNzg4czhnNjcyOHRmZnNxJnV0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZHJpcCZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj1OZXdzbGV0dGVyKy0rQXByaWwrMTUifQ.JbFWpvPTt7ooaBFQFfCoYtwp43ISKgzIyimGhNmRozI) "extensive international corruption record."  
 

> [!FALSE CLAIMS from JBS about net-zero emissions]
> This case, though, is about something else: greenwashing. James claims in a [summons](https://t.dripemail2.com/c/eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..h_kgmhsWd3Yn6YBFcZpem1fLZ7jfaOkd7EzGQY-gA6c) that JBS is misleading consumers with claims it could create "bacon, chicken wings and steak with net-zero emissions." ("Net-zero" means overall they wouldn't produce more of the heat-trapping gases that are driving climate change.) Such ads, James says, "in effect, provide environmentally conscious consumers with a ‘license’ to eat beef."  

The problem, according to James, is that JBS is making these claims even though it knows that it has no feasible way of achieving them.  


## Methane 
She may have a point. For one thing, most of the emissions that come from cattle are in the form of methane, which is a very potent greenhouse gas, especially in the short term. When cows and other ruminants digest carbohydrates, they produce this methane through a process in their stomach known as enteric fermentation. Although people have experimented with various ways to reduce the methane cows emit, such as feeding them seaweed, none have so far proven to be very effective.  

## Land-Use change
And then there is the [land-use change](https://t.dripemail2.com/c/eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..ebO1vE0IpGR6E2Z_b67wPaTMbrDiOjrIZGwZw3G7SyY), which is what the IPCC calls it when a forest is cut down to make way for pasture or other agricultural land. Among the most concerning examples is the destruction of the Amazon rainforest. And as the [largest purchaser](https://t.dripemail2.com/c/eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..-Yll05AD6ySztN5FCcdC_ELN0mTOeYanjgiXgkf2iBM) of cattle from that region, JBS is responsible for much of it.  
 
In fact, in 2021, JBS reported that it was responsible for emitting the equivalent of [71 million tonnes](https://t.dripemail2.com/c/eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..rominFvrYeTEF0KEaxAyUaVhq3kqKTvHn39aCUBqBuU) of CO₂. As James memorably notes in her lawsuit, that’s "more than the total emissions from the country of Ireland during the same year."  
 

This will be an interesting lawsuit to watch. According to a [2023 FAO report](https://t.dripemail2.com/c/eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.-OnRleHQ9SW4lMjAyMDE1JTJDJTIwbGl2ZXN0b2NrLGFudGhyb3BvZ2VuaWMlMjBHSEclMjBlbWlzc2lvbnMifQ.1W0BAuHn4qHET6pH_L9o6CM414VKak2A5eTTcQ4hqpo), livestock production and related land-use change accounted for around 12 percent of all anthropogenic greenhouse gas emissions in 2015. Now this suit will ask a court of law to consider whether there is any technology or farming system that can ever bring those emissions to zero or net-negative.  
 

(You can watch George Monbiot and Allen Savory debate this topic [here](https://t.dripemail2.com/c/eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJhdWQiOiJkZXRvdXIiLCJpc3MiOiJtb25vbGl0aCIsInN1YiI6ImRldG91cl9saW5rIiwiaWF0IjoxNzEzMTkzNDk4LCJuYmYiOjE3MTMxOTM0OTgsImFjY291bnRfaWQiOiIxMjQwNTY4IiwiZGVsaXZlcnlfaWQiOiI2dHdoMGxtdHRtN25iY2V0MGQ3cSIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8veW91dHUuYmUvcmh3RWFDbVEyWEU_c2k9RTd6QmM2ME1xbnZNWTBoZiZfX3M9ejY4MmE3ODhzOGc2NzI4dGZmc3EmdXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kcmlwJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPU5ld3NsZXR0ZXIrLStBcHJpbCsxNSJ9.I60M9H0Sgi2RvlO8W9Wntr6a61WoHSLXtatYjG58Y-g).)

---

## 2025: A Landmark Year for Climate Litigation

2025 marked the tenth anniversary of the Urgenda case, when a Dutch court first ordered a state to cut its emissions faster to protect citizens from climate change. In the decade since, climate litigation has become a global legal architecture. The Guardian counted at least 13 significant wins in 2025 alone, spanning greenwashing verdicts, illegal fossil fuel approvals, and international court rulings.

Key cases that changed the rules:

- **Rosebank and Jackdaw (UK)** — Two North Sea oil and gas fields ruled illegal because the approval process did not account for scope 3 emissions from burning the extracted fuel.
- **TotalEnergies (France)** — A Paris court found TotalEnergies's "reinvention" marketing campaign made misleading net zero claims while continuing fossil fuel expansion.
- **Apple Watch (Germany)** — Frankfurt court ruled Apple could not call its Watch carbon neutral; Apple subsequently dropped the label in other markets too.
- **JBS and Tyson settlements (US)** — JBS paid $1.1 million to New York state; Tyson agreed to drop net zero 2050 claims. Both settled after lawsuits challenged false climate marketing in the meat industry.
- **EnergyAustralia (Australia)** — Settled with Parents for Climate; publicly acknowledged that carbon offsets do not prevent or undo greenhouse gas damage. First Australian greenwashing case against a utility.
- **Three Norwegian oilfields unlawful** — A Norwegian court of appeal declared licences for three North Sea fields illegal for not accounting for full climate impacts.
- **New South Wales coalmine expansion blocked** — Mount Pleasant Optimisation project annulled for failing to account for scope 3 (overseas combustion) emissions.
- **International Court of Justice advisory opinion** — Countries must prevent harm to the climate system; failure could result in compensation obligations.
- **Inter-American Court of Human Rights** — Found there is a human right to a stable climate and states have a duty to protect it.
- **UK forced to publish tougher climate plan** — After its original net zero strategy was ruled unlawful, the government published a revised carbon budget delivery plan committing to decarbonise electricity by 2030.

The pattern is consistent: courts are increasingly holding governments and companies accountable not just for what they emit, but for what they claim. Scope 3 emissions, once invisible in environmental assessments, are now a legal liability.

Source: [The Guardian, Isabella Kaminski, 31 December 2025](../../sources/climate-litigation-wins-2025.md)
